reasoning: The document contains two major defects that require review. The LLM improperly auto-corrected the facility name from 'Roger' to 'Rogers' against the source text, and programmatic detectors identified a full-page background image alongside extracted text, which poses an accessibility issue. Based on the presence of 1-5 major defects, human review is required.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: The output changes the facility's name from 'Roger Energy Complex' to 'Rogers Energy Complex' in two instances on page 2. While the source document itself is inconsistent (using both 'Roger' and 'Rogers' in different places), the pipeline incorrectly altered the specific instances on page 2 to match the pluralized version.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: proposed risk classification for the Roger Energy Complex.
concerns.output_quote: proposed risk classification for the Rogers Energy Complex.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (1): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Energy, Mi
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

action_items: Review the facility name text ('Roger' vs 'Rogers') and correct if exact source fidelity is required
Remove the full-page background image duplicate to ensure clean accessibility
Strip the retained header/footer text from the HTML output
